data.title: Missouri American Water to buy Neosho Water and Wastewater System for $34.5 million, serving 5,400 customers.
data.description: In St. Louis, it was reported today that Missouri American Water, a subsidiary of American Water (NYSE: AWK), has officially signed a deal with the City of Neosho to acquire its water and wastewater systems for $34.5 million. This agreement, which has received unanimous approval from the Neosho City Council, has been submitted to the Missouri Public Service Commission (PSC) for further processing. The Neosho water and wastewater systems serve around 5,400 customer connections. Missouri American Water expressed its enthusiasm for the acquisition.

data.title: SLB to discuss Q3 2025 results on Oct 17; call at 9:30am US Eastern time. Press release at 7am. Call Operator +1 (833) 470-1428 for NA, +1 (646) 844-6383 for others.
data.description: Houston-based SLB (NYSE: SLB) has announced a conference call scheduled for October 17, 2025, to review the third-quarter results ending September 30, 2025. The call will commence at 9:30 am Eastern time, with a press release concerning the results set for 7:00 am Eastern time on the same day. Those interested in participating can join by contacting the Conference Call Operator at +1 (833) 470-1428 within North America or +1 (646) 844-6383 outside of North America.

data.title: La Madeleine introduces new seasonal menu featuring pumpkin dishes and decadent Parisien Hot Chocolate in Dallas, with limited-time offerings.
data.description: A popular French bakery and café in Dallas, la Madeleine, has recently launched a new menu for the fall season. One of the highlights of this menu is the decadent Parisien Hot Chocolate, which has been receiving viral attention. Along with the hot chocolate, the new menu features limited-time offerings such as pumpkin breakfast and brunch entrées. The introduction of these seasonal items aims to attract customers looking for unique and delicious options. The café's initiative has been well-received, with a notable increase in customer interest and engagement.

data.title: Fusion CBD Sleep and Recovery Softgels by Fusion CBD Products target insomnia by regulating the endocannabinoid system.
data.description: A recent report highlights the impact of CBD on the endocannabinoid system, responsible for regulating sleep, mood, and stress levels. The American Academy of Sleep Medicine stresses the crucial role of sleep in maintaining health, emphasizing its significance in cognitive function and overall well-being. The study, conducted in Miami, suggests that CBD's influence on this system could potentially offer benefits in improving sleep quality and managing stress. These findings signify a promising avenue for individuals looking to enhance their sleep patterns and mental wellness.

data.title: Sumo celebrated with events at Japan House London, coinciding with Grand Sumo Tournament at Royal Albert Hall.
data.description: On September 24, 2025, it was reported that in London this October, the Grand Sumo Tournament will be held at the Royal Albert Hall. Along with this event, audiences can attend talks to understand more about the sport and the key roles involved in organizing it. Important numbers related to this news include the date, the location of the tournament, and the mention of informative sessions.

data.title: Wall Street hits record highs after Fed cuts rates by 25 basis points, setting target range at 4%–4.25%. Various indices also closed at all-time highs.
data.description: The financial markets hit fresh record highs on Wall Street following the Federal Reserve's announcement of the first interest rate cut in nine months, raising the possibility of further reductions. The VOO ETF reached new all-time highs during the week. The S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, Dow Jones, and Russell 2000 all closed at record levels, showcasing investor enthusiasm over the Fed's action. At its September meeting, the central bank reduced rates by 25 basis points to a target range of 4%–4.25%, consistent with predictions, amid concerns about a slowing labor market. While not a unanimous decision, Fed Gov. Stephen Miran, appointed by President Trump to replace Adriana Kugler, advocated for the cut. For more details, visit a financial news platform.

data.title: Fed cut rates by 25 bps, signaling 2 more cuts by year-end. Markets adapting to AI and EV breakouts. TSLA, IONQ, & WILD ETF making significant gains. Read more on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Powell hints at possible two more rate cuts by the end of the year, causing ripples in the market. Tesla and IonQ have seen significant surges, with Vista Shares ETF capitalizing on the trend. The fund has shown strong performance so far this year, adapting to capture gains in the AI and EV sectors. The recent Fed rate cut of 25 basis points was in line with expectations, but the real news came from the September Summary of Economic Projections, forecasting two additional cuts by year-end. Powell's remarks have set the tone for market expectations. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BoE holds rates at 4%, US and Canada lower theirs. CPI at 3.8%, unemployment at 4.7%. BoE slows QT program to £70B/year.
data.description: The Bank of England has decided to maintain its key interest rate at 4%, with the Monetary Policy Committee voting 7-2 in favor of the decision. Despite inflation being above target and economic growth being slow, the BoE is cautious in making any changes to the rate. Two members of the MPC argued for a 0.25 percentage point cut to support growth and jobs. UK's unemployment rate stood at 4.7% in the three months before July 2025, while inflation remained at 3.8% in September, the highest among advanced economies. The BoE also announced a reduction in its quantitative tightening program, decreasing government bond sales from £100 billion to £70 billion annually. This move is aimed at shrinking the balance sheet and minimizing disruption in debt markets. The article suggests that the UK government will play a crucial role in economic growth through its tax and spending decisions, as the BoE remains cautious in its approach.

data.title: Stock futures fluctuate ahead of Trump-Xi call. Market reacts to news of China banning tech firms from buying Nvidia chips and a possible TikTok deal. 10-year Treasury bond yields 4.12%, 2-year bond at 3.57%. Federal Reserve likely to cut rates in October meeting with 91.9% probability. Dow Jones -0.01%, S&P 500 0.01%, Nasdaq 100 0.03%, Russell 2000 0.01%. Nasdaq Composite gained over 200 points. CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. up 13% after AI initiatives reveal. U.S. initial jobless claims decline to 231,000. Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index rises to +23.2. Dow Jones ends 124 points higher.
data.description: U.S. stock futures were fluctuating on Friday, awaiting a call between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping. China had banned tech firms from buying Nvidia's chips and NVDA announced an investment in Intel. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.12% and the two-year bond was at 3.57%. Markets priced a 91.9% chance of a rate cut in the October Fed meeting. Futures changed slightly: Dow Jones -0.01%, S&P 500 0.01%, Nasdaq 100 0.03%, Russell 2000 0.01%. The SPY was down 0.074% at $661.79, QQQ up 0.23% at $596.69. Most S&P 500 sectors closed positively, Nasdaq gained 200 points after Fed rate cut. Intel soared 23%, Crowdstrike 13% after announcing growth plans. Jobless claims fell to 231,000, below market estimate, while the Philadelphia Fed index rose to +23.2 in September. Dow ended 0.27% higher.

data.title: EV company's stock rises, then falls due to $1B Bitcoin, Ethereum, and BNB plan by Jiuzi Holdings, drawing mixed reactions.
data.description: Jiuzi Holdings recently revealed their intention to launch a crypto treasury worth $1 billion, with a primary focus on Bitcoin, Ethereum, and BNB. This news has received a varied response from investors, with some showing enthusiasm while others remain cautious.

data.title: MNA nurses and healthcare professionals, along with a Boston University Public Health Professor, to testify on legislation to prevent closures of essential healthcare services due to more than 40 closures since 2009.
data.description: Proposed legislation by members of the MNA aims to maintain crucial healthcare access following the closure of over 40 hospitals and services since 2009. Despite the Department of Public Health recognizing these facilities as vital for community well-being, closures persist. Scheduled for discussion in Boston on Sept. 24, 2025, these bills seek to address the ongoing healthcare crisis and ensure continued access to essential services for the affected communities.

data.title: Subsea 7 S.A. discloses share awards under 2022 Long-Term Incentive Plan approved by Board on September 24, 2025.
data.description: On September 24, 2025, in Luxembourg, Subsea 7 S.A. (Oslo Børs: SUBC, ADR: SUBCY) disclosed the award specifics of its 2022 Long-Term Incentive Plan, sanctioned by the Board.

data.title: Wall Street hits new highs, Oracle sees best rally since 1992. VOO ETF at record levels. Inflation at 2.9%, jobless claims highest since Oct 2021. Labor market weakening.
data.description: This week, Wall Street continued its record-breaking streak fueled by increased investor confidence in Federal Reserve rate cuts and the anticipated growth of AI-driven companies. The VOO ETF has reached all-time high trading levels. Despite concerns about rising inflation and weakening labor market conditions, traders remain optimistic about the prospects of further interest rate reductions. August saw an uptick in annual inflation, reaching 2.9%, the highest level seen since January. Conversely, jobless claims surged to 263,000 in the first week of September, marking the highest weekly figure recorded since October 2021. Following a stagnant August payrolls report, indications suggest a slowdown in the labor market's momentum. The financial markets are...Full details are available on Benzinga.com.
